channel.title: Transcripts: Prof G Pod (Scott Galloway)
channel.description: YouTube video transcripts from Prof G Pod (Scott Galloway)

item.title: Hillary Clinton Accuses GOP of Trump COVER-UP in Epstein Case
item.description:
Jessica Tarlov and Aaron Parnas (@aaronparnas1) break down what the Clintons’ closed-door depositions could actually mean for the Epstein investigation — and what Republicans realistically expect to uncover. JD Vance kicks off his new “war on fraud” by freezing Medicaid funds — is it real reform or 2028 positioning? Plus: FBI shakeups tied to the Trump investigations, escalating tensions with Iran as military strike options are quietly drafted, and Kamala Harris once again teasing a possible presidential run.
Clintons’ depositions in the Epstein investigation JD Vance’s new “war on fraud” If the U.S. is gearing up to strike Iran Whether Kamala Harris really “might” run…again
Follow Jessica Tarlov, @JessicaTarlov Follow Prof G, @TheProfGPod Follow Raging Moderates, @RagingModerates
Subscribe to our YouTube Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@RagingModerates

item.title: Trump Sets Up JD Vance to Fail?
item.description: JD Vance makes his first big move as “Fraud Czar” by suspending $259M in Medicaid funds to Minnesota. Jessica Tarlov and Aaron Parnas discuss what this all means, on Raging Moderates.

item.title: A Newsom/Harris Ticket?
item.description: Jessica Tarlov and Aaron Parnas discuss whether or not Kamala Harris is gearing up for a 2028 presidential run, on Raging Moderates.
item.content:encoded:
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/AnKwoQeGscA

Transcript

Kla Harris was on the partner's perspective. What did she say about potentially running in 2028? >> Well, as any good politician, uh, she did not announce her candidacy for 2028 on my show. I know it sucks. >> I do think she's going to run though based on >> Really? >> Yeah, I think she'll run. And personally, I think she'll announce by the end of the year. I think she's going to try to do like a Trump 2.0 of like announce right after the midterms. I think that she is to some degree just buying herself time and buying herself time as the front runner when she might not be running. Like I feel like she's not going to run.

>> Listen, if she doesn't run, it's it kind of seems like Newsome's inevitability at this point unless something radically changes. If she does run, then I think it's a two-headed kind of race. >> California monster >> Newsome Harris 2028. >> Who stabs who first? >> Oh, I I would not support that ticket. And no, >> I'm kidding. Like, but I'm just saying like that would never happen. Andre to have to talk about California politics >> incessantly for two years would be hideous.

item.title: BREAKING — Trump Bombs Iran
item.description: Scott Galloway and Jessica Tarlov go live to discuss the morning's news that Trump has conducted major military strikes in Iran.

item.title: BREAKING — Trump Bombs Iran
item.description: Scott Galloway and Jessica Tarlov go live to discuss the morning's news that Trump has conducted major military strikes in Iran.

item.title: BIG ANNOUNCEMENT from Scott and Jessica
item.description:
BREAKING NEWS: Raging Moderates is going five days a week!
New episodes drop every weekday evening with Scott Galloway and Jessica Tarlov breaking down the day’s biggest political stories—smart analysis, facts and data first, and yes… a healthy dose of rage against the extremes.
Subscribe on your desired platform:
YouTube: @RagingModerates
Apple Podcasts: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/raging-moderates-with-scott-galloway-and-jessica-tarlov/id1774505095
Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/2Kh9t2fiR7kE601GH36ajN?si=58afb98f60264f5a.
item.content:encoded:
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NQmB88Q9D8c

Transcript

I'm Scott Galloway. >> And I'm Jessica Tarliff. >> On Raging Moderates, Jess and I are stuck in the middle with you and we're mad. Politics is changing this country fast and many in the media haven't kept up. >> The Republican party has been overtaken by the MAGA movement. That's nothing new. I see it up close and personal every day when I'm on the five. >> Yeah, I hate this script. Anyways, so the bottom line is we're in the middle and everyone hates us, but >> but we love us. >> Yeah. But we've decided that there's a reason why more people identify as moderates now or as independents. And

what I would say to our fellow moderate brothers and sisters, here's the bottom line. Are you a critical thinker? Do you go issue by issue? And do you recognize that the majority of America agrees on at least some reasonable sense of the majority of the issues? So if you want to come together, come talk to Jess and Scott. We like to think of ourselves as reasonable. I know that people who identify as moderates are getting a lot of flack right now. I see that in every focus group. You know, everyone wants a fighter, not a moderate, blah blah blah. We're not mushy anymore. It doesn't work that way. Ruben Ggo is a moderate. John Oaf is a moderate. Gavin Newsome is a

moderate. It's not the dirty word that you think. Whatever survey you're looking at, people are upset about the extremes in our politics. Nobody likes the extreme left, extreme right. They want some kind of consensus, but they don't want to give up the fight. And that's exactly where raging moderates lives. We rage against those extremes. Are we lefties? Definitely. Are we going to vote for Democrats? Yeah, we better win. And by big margins, but we're not nutcases. That is my solemn vow to you. >> And if you're a algorithm, you're going to hate this show. >> The reason that we're telling you this is we're going 5 days a week. >> That's right. Every day, Jess is leveraging our contacts on the Hill. I'm

leveraging my profanity to bring you something that's both informative and entertaining. >> So, if any of this resonates with you, even the tiniest bit, listen to Raging Moderates now 5 days a week. Subscribe to Raging Moderates now on YouTube and on Spotify, Apple Podcast, or anywhere you get your podcast. Be a critical thinker. >> New episodes come out every weekday evening for your commute home. You'll get smart political analysis on the day's biggest stories and discussions based on facts and data. And yeah, there's a good amount of rage guaranteed. >> Be hated by the extreme left and the extreme right. It's fine. >> We really are.

>> We're here for you. >> All five days. >> All five days. That's right. All five days. >> All five days. Tune in.

item.title: “I’m…very afraid of a prolonged war”
item.description: Trump once campaigned on ending “forever wars,” but new military action against Iran suggests a major shift. Scott Galloway and Jessica Tarlov discuss, on Raging Moderates.
item.content:encoded:
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/2NRjDKNWaxc

Transcript

President Trump is calling it a massive and ongoing targeting of Iran's nuclear program, missile industry, navy, and even signaling support for regime change. Are we about to enter a forever war with boots on the ground? >> The thing that feels different about this from other actions that President Trump has taken in both the first term and the second term is that he actually gave a speech where he said there may be American casualties. and he campaigned both times on being the peace president. He loves to take a victory lap on being

right about Iraq and Afghanistan. And this was something that was really uniting for both the right and the left that people don't want to be in these wars anymore. And you're seeing veterans from across the political spectrum who are about my age who signed up after 9/11 to go and risk their lives saying, "What's the deal here? Haven't we seen this movie before?" So I am conflicted at the moment but very afraid of the prolonged war. >> The Straits of Hormuz have not been under a constant level of havoc or or or siege. You haven't seen missiles flying into Israel from Hezbollah. You haven't

seen all this sort of insecurity and quite frankly terror. Uh Syria has been decapitated. Russia is no longer a threat there because they've lost their proxy in Syria. The hands of Iran have basically been cut off. And I would argue that uh this is, if you will, a bit of a blinking green light that we should finish the job.

item.title: “The Trump administration is in retreat”
item.description: Donald Trump addressed the nation with his rationale for bombing Iran, but Jessica Tarlov isn’t buying it — on Raging Moderates.

item.title: Trump LOSES CONTROL as UNLAWFUL War in Iran ERUPTS
item.description:
Scott Galloway and Jessica Tarlov are joined by retired U.S. Navy Admiral James Stavridis to break down a rapidly escalating crisis in the Middle East. Following U.S. and Israeli strikes that killed Iran’s Supreme Leader, Tehran has formed a transitional council and is vowing retaliation. U.S. troops are dead. Missiles are flying between Iran, Israel, and American bases across the Gulf. The Strait of Hormuz is under threat, the price of oil is spiking, and global markets are on edge. Is there an off-ramp — or are we at the start of a wider war? What does regime decapitation actually accomplish? How might Iran respond — cyberattacks, proxies, broader regional escalation? And what does this mean for oil prices, the global economy, China, and President Trump’s legacy?
#scottgalloway #jessicatarlov #trump #iran #war #ayatollahalikhamenei #supremeleader #israel #netanyahu #marcorubio #petehegseth #nuclearweapons #tehran
Follow Jessica Tarlov, @JessicaTarlov Follow Prof G, @TheProfGPod Follow Raging Moderates, @RagingModerates
Subscribe to our YouTube Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@RagingModerates

item.title: “Early stages of a very long war?”
item.description: Admiral James Stavridis joins Scott Galloway and Jessica Tarlov to discuss potential outcomes for the current conflict in Iran, on Raging Moderates.
item.content:encoded:
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/4HeSv65TbJg

Transcript

Admiral, from a military standpoint, is there a clear offramp here, or are we looking at the very early stages of a longer war in the Middle East? >> I'll give you three scenarios very quickly. First one is the happy scenario. This would be where the people of Iran rise up and overthrow the revolutionary guard. I think sadly that's a 30% chance at best. The other 70% breaks into two scenarios. one, you could kind of think of it as Iran 3.0 that includes some remnants of this

government, but uh that behaves in a more moderate, sensible fashion. And then there's kind of the dark end of the spectrum in that 70% tunch, and that's that team moola Ayatollah toughs it out, takes the bombing, no boots on the ground are coming, and they repress the populace even worse. So there's a snapshot of the future as I see it.
